findings_none: Every re-executed check is green on the candidate tree, an exhaustive token-survival audit of the finalize/adapt deletions found every load-bearing mechanic surviving on-surface or in its delegated script/agent home, the six mirrors per topic diverge only by forge nouns with the pre-existing forge framing byte-unchanged, the fast-compliance-backstop chain leaves only two retirement comments plus CHANGELOG/decision-record history, the five validators are byte-untouched so no pin was obliterated, no provenance token entered any of the 12 surfaces, and both additive-suite failure sets are identical to base.

## Review narrative

What I read first: the canonical review context at kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/review-contexts/e16727af603861003036b75e30166e77526402f6f0a485fd5501ded3a4f88aa2.json (confirmed: validation_obligations is empty, review_phase discovery, no prior findings, no repair delta, claim_root_base commit a94ea0b7), then n3's evidence (nonce 6b82c068ed13 echoed above) and n2's approved verdict for context. The candidate slice is exactly the 15 working-tree modifications on top of a94ea0b7 (verified: git status shows 15 modified files, all inside n3's 21-file frozen write set; the 6 files n3 declared UNCHANGED — the five contract validators and test-kimi-edition.js — are indeed absent from the diff, and all untracked paths are harness-owned run state).

Re-execution (all from the worktree, exit codes captured directly): root validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; cmp of scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js against plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0 (byte-twin identical); validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; the gitlab and gitea contract validators exit 0; test-route-reachability.js exit 0 with 2276 assertions (one fewer than n2's 2277, exactly the removed SUPERSET-PROOF pair — the deleted fn-fast-compliance-backstop manifest block only shrinks the single aggregate obligated-count assert, so a net minus-one is arithmetically coherent); simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js exit 0 with the sentinel; all four --forbidden-only spot checks on the gitlab/gitea finalize and adapt command mirrors passed. For the seeded run gap I re-established base myself by git-archive-extracting HEAD into scratch: opencode at base is 385 passed with the single H1 (#F3 hookPath) failure, on the candidate 383 passed with the identical failure line — the minus-2 is exactly the two intentionally removed A20 asserts, no new failure; kimi FATALs identically both sides (ENOENT on the Phase-C-retired pre-commit hook; outputs byte-identical after path normalization, and test-kimi-edition.js plus sync-kimi-edition.js are untouched). The gate claim does not assert those suites green and the failure sets are unchanged.

Finalize walk-through: I read the full trimmed commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md (453 lines) and walked the complete worktree finalize from it alone. The replan fence, four-gate barrier bash (pinned VALIDATOR resolution plus the four --*-check invocations with directly captured exit codes and KAOLA_FINALIZE_BASE forwarding), dual-mode Validation Gate (chains_unverified/chains_stale/chains_red with remedies and the receipt-freshness ordering — run-chains as the last pre-Finalization action after all test-consumed prose lands, with the reuse-boundary honesty rule), Run-Gap Sweep, Resume Detection routing table, staging guard, sink-metadata capture (SINK_ISSUE_NUMBERS bundle closure included), contractor dispatch with attestation boundary and crash recovery, Step 9 sink with the merge/pr cases, exit-3 pivot, the script-owned --sink worktree transaction, co-tenant merge protocol, crash-resume journals, exit-code table, closure-audit sweep, and the keep-open partial-close lane (merge-sink-only, exit-3 refusal under keep-open, roadmap preservation) all survive as actionable bullets — the worktree sequence (feature commit, receipt, cmdFinalize --keep-worktree, push, sink-merge --sink) is fully reconstructible. Beyond reading, I ran a mechanical token-survival audit: every backticked identifier, flag, and typed token deleted from the canonical finalize command was checked for survival on the after surface. All residual "gone" tokens are benign: line-wrap artifacts (Documentation Update Checklist, git status --short --branch), retired-fast-path residue (fast_compliance_unresolved, summary-write, phase5-review.md, the chains.json opt-in), script-internal narration (--force-with-lease inside the sink transaction, headSha legacy fallback, refusal payload field names), or content delegated to its sole procedural home in agents/contractor.md (commit-gate mechanics). Two I chased to code: the dropped "## Commit And Push" summary-template section's only consumer is a swallow-on-error literal replacement in cmdFinalize (scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2161) with nothing asserting the section, and the READY FOR FINAL GIT GATE sentinel survives in trimmed Step 5; and the contractor prompt's --keep-issue-open is an accepted cmdFinalize alias (scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:165) with flag guidance identical to the base prompt. The adapt walk likewise completes from the 211-line command alone: entry guard with git-freshness gating and co-tenant clean-check, planner-first control boundary, the claim-escalate verdict routing (refuse hard-stop versus escalate ask), the bounded plan_invalid repair loop, TodoWrite task-list construction, plan-run handoff, bundle lane with its typed refusals and KAOLA_BUNDLE_MAX_ISSUES default, and the question/bug-shaped investigation section.

Six-mirror convergence: word-level diffs of canonical versus gitlab and gitea for both command topics show exclusively forge-noun and script-path substitutions (a zero-residue filter after excluding forge vocabulary), and the three command mirrors are line-count identical (453/453/453 and 211/211/211). The three adapt SKILLs differ from each other only by forge nouns. The three finalize SKILLs carry pre-existing forge-specific contractor-choreography framing (issue_iid, worktree-cleanup atomicity notes); I proved n3 did not move that needle — the cross-forge divergence residue is byte-identical at base and after for both forge pairs, meaning the 94-line trim applied uniformly. The reviewer-contract-v2-finalization block (21 lines) and reviewer-contract-v2-authoring block (32 lines) are byte-identical across all six surfaces of their topics by direct extract-and-cmp, and that identity is machine-pinned (root validator line 1263 plus its cross-six byte-identity block; T17 in scripts/test-route-reachability.js:632).

Fast-compliance dangling-reference sweep: a whole-tree case-insensitive grep across all editions including templates/opencode and templates/kimi finds only CHANGELOG.md history, the docs/decisions/D-530-01.md record, and the two retirement comments n3 documented (scripts/test-opencode-edition.js:531, scripts/test-route-reachability.js:433). No PIN marker, manifest entry, SUPERSET pair, or assert survives anywhere. Pin audit: the five validators are byte-untouched in this diff, so nothing was obliterated — the only pin removals are the three-part fast-compliance chain (manifest block, SUPERSET pair, A20), and every remaining validator pin passes against the trimmed surfaces, proving the diet preserved each load-bearing token. Provenance: the only hash-token matches on all 12 surfaces are the issue #N / issue #<N> runtime placeholders, which the provenance rule explicitly exempts.

AC-D miss-reason honesty: both recorded reasons are arithmetically sound. The T17 commonTokens list is exactly 14 entries as claimed, and replan_planner_attestation_invalid (dropped from the finalize command) is a planner-profile token, not a command commonToken, so T17 green is consistent. The finalize command's structural floor measures 134 fenced-code lines, 24 headings, and 71 blanks, plus the 21-line byte-frozen finalization block — roughly 250 lines of mandatory apparatus, leaving about 220 prose lines; reaching 300 would require deleting most of the remaining Band-1 step mechanics, so 453 is credibly at the honest floor. The adapt command's 32-line byte-frozen authoring block, replan fence, claim-escalate block, bundle lane, and question/bug section total near the claimed 140 mandatory lines against a 150 target, leaving effectively zero connective prose — an honest miss. The adapt SKILLs' line-neutral 465 is defensible: their extra mass is codex-side authoring reference the command legitimately delegates to agents/workflow-planner.md, and their one hunk (the co-tenant compression) preserves the Band-1 kernel.

Two pre-existing, non-blocking observations for visibility (both present at base a94ea0b7, semantically carried not introduced): the adapt command intro still calls fast/full "explicit path-name escapes" although they are now refused with path_not_installed, and the unchanged adapt SKILL paragraph still says "the same as full/fast paths". Neither is candidate-caused; a later prose sweep could retire both phrases.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: The n3 finalize/adapt diet preserves every Band-1 finalize, sink, keep-open, and authoring mechanic verifiably, the six mirrors per topic remain convergent with pre-existing forge framing untouched, the fast-compliance-backstop chain is removed with zero dangling references, no validator pin was weakened, provenance stayed out of all twelve surfaces, all re-executed gates are green with both additive-suite failure sets unchanged from base, and the recorded line-target miss reasons are arithmetically honest, so this gate approves the candidate.
